figures.caption: Fig. 2: End-to-end execution of a narrative beat for the Emma persona. The diagram illustrates the Plan-Retrieve-Generate cycle. The resulting evidence is synthesized into a narrative segment, grounded in the retrieved facts.

Table 3: Readability and Cohesion metrics by persona, narrative length, and RAG variant. FRE denotes the Flesch-Kinkaid Reading Ease (higher is easier). Glob/Loc Cohesion is cosine similarity (higher is more cohesive).
Table 2: Factual grounding metrics (Support and Coverage, in %) by persona, length, and RAG variant. Higher is better. KG-RAG demonstrates the highest support, while Hybrid-RAG excels in coverage.
Fig. 1: Story construction as a list of beats based on a shared CQ pool. Each beat is realised by retrieving CQ answers and prompting an LLM in beat order.
